It is associated with the Impressionists.
It is associated with Mozart's Don Giovanni.
It is associated with Jean-Baptiste Faure.
It is associated with Halévy's father Ludovic's Monsieur Cardinal.
It is associated with Paul Gavarni.
It is associated with Alfred Grévin.
ConsistsOf: It consists of singers performing a scene from the opera Don Giovanni.
It consists of singers wearing predominantly orange and yellow costumes.
It consists of patrons in two box seats overlooking the stage.
It consists of a pastel drawn over an earlier monotype.
CreatedBy: It was created by Edgar Degas.
HasA: It has a Degas signature at lower left.
It has singers with open mouths.
It has singers with hands extended towards the audience.
It has patrons in two box seats overlooking the stage.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the influence of French caricaturists of the era.
It is characterized by singers' "hideous" faces.
It is characterized by the Impressionist aesthetic.
It is characterized by a lack of detail in the faces of the singers in the foreground.
It is characterized by being a pastel drawn over an earlier monotype.
It is characterized by depicting an operatic performance without dancers.
It is characterized by its rectangular, almost square shape.
It is characterized by its dimensions of 27 centimetres high by 32 centimetres wide.
HeldIn: It was held in the Musée du Luxembourg in Paris.
It was held in the Louvre.
It was held in the Musée d'Orsay.
It was held in the Musée Cantini in Marseille.
It was held in New York.
It was held in San Francisco.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by French caricaturists of the era.
It was influenced by Honoré Daumier.
It was influenced by Paul Gavarni.
It was influenced by Alfred Grévin.
IsA: It is a pastel on monotype.
It is part of a series of similar works depicting daily public entertainment at the time.
It is a work of modern art.
KnownAs: It is known as "The Chorus Singers".
It is known as Chorus d'Opéra.
KnownFor: It is known for depicting an operatic performance without dancers.
It is known for its Impressionist aesthetic.
It is known for being stolen and recovered.
MemberOf: It is a member of the collection of the Musée du Luxembourg.
It is a member of the Louvre collection.
It is a member of the Musée d'Orsay collection.
It is a member of French Impressionist heritage.

OccurredIn: It occurred in 1877.
